title: Remote Session Duration Anomalies
spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="vpn:session" earliest=-7d
| eval dur_hrs=(end_time-start_time)/3600
| eventstats median(dur_hrs) as med, stdev(dur_hrs) as sd by user
| eval z=if(sd>0, (dur_hrs-med)/sd, 0)
| where abs(z)>3 OR dur_hrs>48 OR dur_hrs<0.01
| table user, dur_hrs, med, z, src
description: Statistical outliers in VPN session length ŌĆö unusually short (brute probe) or long (unattended tunnel) vs UC-17.3.8 fixed thresholds.
value: Statistical outliers in VPN session length ŌĆö unusually short (brute probe) or long (unattended tunnel) vs UC-17.3.8 fixed thresholds.
implementation: Requires reliable `start_time`/`end_time`. Tune z-score or use `anomalydetection`.

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ŌĆö correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: VPN TA.
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: `vpn:session` or ASA with start/end.
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
Requires reliable `start_time`/`end_time`. Tune z-score or use `anomalydetection`.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="vpn:session" earliest=-7d
| eval dur_hrs=(end_time-start_time)/3600
| eventstats median(dur_hrs) as med, stdev(dur_hrs) as sd by user
| eval z=if(sd>0, (dur_hrs-med)/sd, 0)
| where abs(z)>3 OR dur_hrs>48 OR dur_hrs<0.01
| table user, dur_hrs, med, z, src
```

Understanding this SPL

**Remote Session Duration Anomalies** ŌĆö Statistical outliers in VPN session length ŌĆö unusually short (brute probe) or long (unattended tunnel) vs UC-17.3.8 fixed thresholds.

Documented **Data sources**: `vpn:session` or ASA with start/end. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): VPN TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vpn; **sourcetype**: vpn:session. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Scopes the data: index=vpn, sourcetype="vpn:session", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
ŌĆó `eval` defines or adjusts **dur_hrs** ŌĆö often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
ŌĆó `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
ŌĆó `eval` defines or adjusts **z** ŌĆö often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
ŌĆó Filters the current rows with `where abs(z)>3 OR dur_hrs>48 OR dur_hrs<0.01` ŌĆö typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
ŌĆó Pipeline stage (see **Remote Session Duration Anomalies**): table user, dur_hrs, med, z, src

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Scatter (duration vs time), Table (outliers), Histogram (duration).
grandmaExplanation: Statistical outliers in remote access session length ŌĆö unusually short (brute probe) or long (unattended tunnel) vs UC-17.3.8 fixed thresholds ŌĆö so unsafe traffic and policy bypasses are caught quickly.
